leads.gmbSignals: Overpass/OSM public listing surfaced shop=car_repair, Oskaloosa address 1603 South Market Street, phone +1-641-673-3310, and a listed website URL at arnoldmotorsupply.com/location/howards-auto-repair.php. Google rating not visible in this pass.
leads.contactRoute: phone/manual research; no public email found
verifiedEvidence: Direct fetch of http://arnoldmotorsupply.com/location/howards-auto-repair.php redirected to https://arnoldmotorsupply.com:443/location/howards-auto-repair.php and returned HTTP 404.
Fetched page title was '404 Error - Arnold Motor Supply'.
Public listing phone +1-641-673-3310 and Oskaloosa address signal were present in Overpass/OSM data.
leads.diagnosis: A public listing website click appears to land on a third-party 404 page, which can block mobile searchers before they see shop context, phone confidence, or a repair-service path.
leads.bestHook: Helpful alert: the listed public website path is returning a 404, and the sample shows the cleaner replacement path.
leads.whyThisShouldClose: The problem is specific, visible, and non-judgmental: fix a dead public path in a high-intent auto repair category.
leads.recommendedChannel: manual phone script or researched email only
leads.mockupDecisionReason: A visual concept strengthens the bug note because it shows exactly what the dead listing path could become.
leads.designDifferentiation: Warm repair-ticket / service-counter layout with a 404 repair-ticket motif; differs from recent dark mechanic pages and avoids fake ASE badges, fake reviews, and right-side quote forms.
unverifiedClaimsAvoided: exact services beyond auto repair listing
leads.visualQaNotes: Browser visual QA passed locally: credible, distinct repair-ticket/service-counter concept; sample framing clear; no desktop layout breakage; mobile sticky call dock present in CSS. Minor optional polish: reduce ticket-card tilt/shadow or clarify secondary CTA before production.
leads.subject: quick 404 note for Howard's Auto Repair
leads.draftCopy:
Hi Howard's Auto Repair team — I found a public website link for Howard's that opened to a 404 page on an Arnold Motor Supply URL instead of a shop page. If customers are clicking that from search, they may not see your phone or repair info before choosing who to call.

I made a no-obligation sample showing how that first screen could look with your public phone and Oskaloosa context: https://sybeorg-mockups.vercel.app/howards-auto-repair/

Worth polishing this up for you? Reply NO and I will not follow up.

leads.gmbSignals: Overpass/OSM public listing surfaced shop=car_repair, Waterloo address 357 Fletcher Avenue, phone +1-319-232-0854, listed website http://xpressoilandlube.com, and hours Mo-Fr 08:00-17:00; Sa 09:00-15:00. Google rating not visible in this pass.
leads.contactRoute: phone/manual research; no public email found
verifiedEvidence: Direct fetch of http://xpressoilandlube.com failed DNS resolution during this pass.
Public listing phone +1-319-232-0854 and hours Mo-Fr 08:00-17:00; Sa 09:00-15:00 were present in Overpass/OSM data.
leads.diagnosis: If the listed domain is still attached to search/local listings, customers may hit a dead end before seeing hours, phone, or the fastest way to call from mobile.
leads.bestHook: Helpful alert: the listed domain did not resolve; the concept shows a simple replacement page with phone, hours, and service paths.
leads.whyThisShouldClose: A dead domain is an easy-to-understand leak; the business has phone and hours data to make a useful first page without inventing claims.
leads.recommendedChannel: manual phone script or researched email only
leads.mockupDecisionReason: A visual sample is useful because the existing domain path does not load and a minimum useful replacement can be shown clearly.
leads.designDifferentiation: Dark bay-status board and fast-lube routing system; differs from Howard's warm repair-ticket page and recent generic auto-shop layouts.
leads.visualQaNotes: Browser visual QA passed locally: credible, distinct bay-status-board concept; sample framing clear; no desktop layout breakage; mobile sticky call dock present in CSS. Minor optional polish: tighten hero headline/alert-card spacing before production.
leads.subject: quick domain note for Xpress Oil & Lube
leads.draftCopy:
Hi Xpress Oil & Lube team — I checked the public domain I found for Xpress Oil & Lube and it did not resolve during my pass. If that URL is still connected to a customer-facing listing, drivers may hit a dead end before they see your phone or hours.

I made a no-obligation sample of a simple replacement page with the public phone, Waterloo context, and a mobile call path: https://sybeorg-mockups.vercel.app/xpress-oil-lube-waterloo/

Worth polishing this up for you? Reply NO and I will not follow up.

leads.gmbSignals: Overpass/OSM public listing surfaced shop=car_repair, phone +1 712-943-7830 and +1 712-587-9287, and hours Mo-Fr 07:30-17:00; Sa-Su off; no website/email fields in this pass.
leads.contactRoute: phone/manual research; no public email found
verifiedEvidence: Public OSM listing contained phone/hours but no website/email fields in this pass.
leads.diagnosis: Potential local-profile leak: drivers can see phone/hours, but no public website path was exposed in this data pass, so there is no place to explain services or convert after-hours visitors.
leads.bestHook: Profile/contact-path cleanup and first-site concept after confirming active listing details.
leads.whyThisShouldClose: Auto repair is high-intent and the no-website signal is concrete, but contact details need manual confirmation before a mockup-led pitch.
leads.mockupDecisionReason: Skipped; no real assets or public email yet, so a mockup would be mostly generic.
leads.subject: quick local listing idea for Pioneer Auto
leads.draftCopy:
Hi Pioneer Auto team — I came across a Sergeant Bluff public listing with phone/hours, but in the listing data I checked I did not see a website attached. If that is still true, drivers may have no easy place to confirm services or reach you after hours from search.

I would want to verify the details first, but a simple one-page site/profile cleanup could close that gap. Worth polishing an example for you? Reply NO and I will not follow up.

leads.gmbSignals: Nominatim/OSM public listing surfaced roofing category, Ames address, website, phone +1 515-708-4027, public email ge.construction.ia@gmail.com, and hours Mo-Sa 08:00-18:00. Google rating not visible in this pass.
leads.contactRoute: public email from OSM listing and official-site mailto; contact form also present
leads.emailSource: OSM public listing plus official-site mailto in fetched HTML
verifiedEvidence: Official site loaded HTTP 200 with title 'Super Eagle Roofing - #1 Roofing Ames IA Company'.
Official-site fetch exposed mailto ge.construction.ia@gmail.com and storm/roof repair keywords.
leads.diagnosis: Functional website and reachable email, but the easiest close is not a redesign: create a storm/roof-repair landing page that is more ad-ready and focused than the general site.
leads.bestHook: Offer one focused Ames roof repair/storm page rather than a full website critique.
leads.whyThisShouldClose: Public email route is strong, roofing has high-ticket seasonal economics, and the pitch can be additive instead of insulting their current site.
leads.mockupDecisionReason: Skipped; existing site is functional and a generic roofing mockup would be weaker than a verified service-page concept built after deeper page audit.
leads.subject: quick storm/roof page idea for Super Eagle Roofing
leads.draftCopy:
Hi Super Eagle Roofing team — I came across your Ames roofing site and email while researching local roof repair/storm-damage pages. Your site already loads, so I would not lead with a redesign pitch. The clearer opportunity is a focused roof-repair or storm-inspection landing page that can be used for seasonal search traffic or ads.

If useful, I can mock up one focused page using only verified details from your site. Worth polishing this up for you? Reply NO and I will not follow up.

leads.whyThisShouldClose: Paintless dent/hail repair has clear seasonal demand and the business has a reachable public email route.

leads.subject: quick hail repair page idea for Midwest Dent
leads.draftCopy:
Hi Midwest Dent team — I found your Clinton dent/hail repair site and public email while researching local auto-service pages. I would not lead with a generic redesign; the more useful angle looks like a focused hail-repair landing page that makes the appointment path easier when demand spikes.

If useful, I can put together a sample page using verified info from your site and leave room for your real photos/proof. Worth polishing this up for you? Reply NO and I will not follow up.
